title: Natural Language Processing with Transformers
subtitle: Building Language Applications with Hugging Face
author: Lewis Tunstall, Leandro von Werra, Thomas Wolf
themes: Applying pretrained transformer models to real-world NLP tasks
Mastering the Hugging Face ecosystem (Transformers, Datasets, Tokenizers, Accelerate)
Encoder versus decoder architectures and when to choose each
Practical fine-tuning strategies on consumer and cloud hardware
Bridging the gap between research notebooks and deployable applications
Democratizing modern NLP by hiding PyTorch/TensorFlow boilerplate
An honest look at data scarcity, evaluation, and future directions
awards: Amazon #1 New Release in AI & Machine Learning (2022)
O'Reilly bestseller in Data and AI categories
Adopted as a primary reference in multiple university NLP courses
excerpt:
Since its introduction in 2017, the Transformer architecture has become
    the foundation of modern natural language processing. This practical
    book — written by the team that built the Hugging Face Transformers
    library — shows you how to train, fine-tune, and deploy state-of-the-art
    models for text classification, named entity recognition, question
    answering, text generation, and summarization. You'll learn the full
    Hugging Face ecosystem hands-on: Transformers, Datasets, Tokenizers, and
    Accelerate, with code that runs on your laptop or scales to the cloud.
